records.ety: [OF. estoper to stop, plug, close, F. touper, LL. stuppare to close with tow, obstruct, fr. L. stuppa tow, oakum, cf. Gr. sty`pph. Cf. Stop.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Estophed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Estopping.]

defs: To impede or bar by estoppel.
quotes: A party will be estopped by his admissions, where his intent is to influence another, or derive an advantage to himself.
